feedback.text: As a creator who depends on the internet to make a living, as well as someone who engages with various hobbies such as playing videogames, there are several things that trouble me these days. The tendency for large payment processors, who have an effective stranglehold on the flow of online economy, to pressure marketplaces and individual retailers into only allowing what these processors find acceptable, for instance. Completely legal interactions between consenting adults are stymied according to repressive corporate policy in a clear attempt to stifle EU citizens' rights to free speech and self-expression. And more often than not these regressive terms of service are deliberately left vague to further broaden their impact. LGBT+ people online are especially vulnerable, as these terms are often interpreted as broadly as possible, labeling queer people like myself as obscene and essentially attempting to push us out of public and economic view. Another troubling thing is the issue of digital archival. Games especially are vulnerable to becoming lost media. Though a young medium, video games are still a valid art form, and there should be a means of preserving these programs for posterity. Not just for cultural reasons, but for technical as well. Further troubling issues are the, frankly deranged, pushes by corporations and organizations to integrate so-called 'AI' into as many things as possible these days. Despite the fact that these programs and services wreak havoc on the environment due to abominable energy usage, as well as the fact that many of these LLMs and image generators are based on copyrighted and trademarked images and information that has been illegally acquired and reproduced by these systems. They also simply do not work as advertised for the most part, and forcing such a widespread reliance on terribly unreliable systems is a recipe for disaster.
